rawInput.symptom: I have cloudy urine with a bad smell and I have not been drinking water all day, feeling dizzy and tired

structuredInput.symptomText: I have cloudy urine with a bad smell and I have not been drinking water all day, feeling dizzy and tired

analysisResult.summary: Based on the signals provided, the most likely concern is Dehydration (medium risk).
recommendedActions: Drink at least 8 glasses (2 litres) of water daily
Use electrolyte drinks if sweating or vomiting
Avoid diuretics like caffeine during recovery
Monitor urine colour — aim for pale straw yellow
Consult a doctor for possible antibiotic treatment
Increase fluid intake — water and unsweetened cranberry juice
Avoid caffeine and alcohol until symptoms resolve
Do not delay treatment; untreated UTIs can reach the kidneys
analysisResult.disclaimer: This is not medical advice. Seek professional care for serious or worsening symptoms. If you are experiencing an emergency, call 911 or your local emergency number immediately.

rawInput.symptom: severe chest pain and difficulty breathing, I cannot breathe

structuredInput.symptomText: severe chest pain and difficulty breathing, I cannot breathe

analysisResult.summary: Emergency signals detected. Immediate medical attention required.
recommendedActions: 🚨 CALL 911 OR YOUR LOCAL EMERGENCY NUMBER IMMEDIATELY
Do not drive yourself — call for emergency assistance
Stay calm and stay on the line with emergency services
Rest and avoid physical exertion
Use a humidifier and stay hydrated
Consult a doctor if breathing difficulty persists
analysisResult.disclaimer: This is not medical advice. Seek professional care for serious or worsening symptoms. If you are experiencing an emergency, call 911 or your local emergency number immediately.

rawInput.symptom: bad back pain flank pain foamy urine swollen ankles

structuredInput.symptomText: bad back pain flank pain foamy urine swollen ankles

analysisResult.summary: Based on the signals provided, the most likely concern is Kidney Stress / Kidney Issue (high risk).
recommendedActions: Schedule a kidney function test (blood creatinine + urine albumin)
Monitor urine output and colour daily
Reduce sodium and avoid NSAID pain relievers
Stay well hydrated unless otherwise directed by your doctor
Consult a doctor for possible antibiotic treatment
Increase fluid intake — water and unsweetened cranberry juice
Avoid caffeine and alcohol until symptoms resolve
Do not delay treatment; untreated UTIs can reach the kidneys
analysisResult.disclaimer: This is not medical advice. Seek professional care for serious or worsening symptoms. If you are experiencing an emergency, call 911 or your local emergency number immediately.
